features.summary: Johnson & Johnson reported strong Q4 2024 results with worldwide sales of $22.5 billion, up 6.7% operationally. Full year 2024 sales reached $88.8 billion with 5.9% growth, achieving their $57 billion target a year early. Innovative Medicine delivered $14.3 billion in Q4 with 10 key brands showing double-digit growth, while MedTech generated $8.2 billion with 7.6% growth. The company completed significant M&A activity including Shockwave, V-Wave, and announced the Intra-Cellular Therapies acquisition, representing over $32 billion in strategic investments.
features.management_outlook: For 2025, J&J expects operational sales growth of 2.5-3.5% (midpoint 3.0%) despite headwinds from STELARA biosimilar competition and Part D redesign impacts of approximately $2 billion. The company projects strong adjusted operational EPS growth of 8.7% at midpoint, demonstrating resilience through diversification. Management anticipates higher growth in the second half of 2025 versus first half, with new product launches including TREMFYA in IBD and RYBREVANT plus LAZCLUZE in lung cancer driving momentum.
features.risks: Key risks include accelerating STELARA biosimilar erosion throughout 2025, temporary pause of VARIPULSE procedures in the US due to safety investigation, and continued macroeconomic pressures in China affecting MedTech performance. The company faces competitive pressures in US electrophysiology from rival PFA technologies and potential revenue recognition challenges. Currency headwinds are expected to negatively impact reported results by approximately $1.7 billion or 2.0%.
features.key_drivers: Growth will be driven by the multiple myeloma franchise with DARZALEX exceeding $3 billion quarterly sales, CARVYKTI approaching $1 billion annually, and bispecifics TECVAYLI and TALVEY gaining traction. SPRAVATO surpassed $1 billion in annual sales with 45% growth and received monotherapy approval for treatment-resistant depression. MedTech growth supported by Abiomed's 13% growth, Shockwave integration contributing $258 million, and continued innovation across cardiovascular, vision, and surgical portfolios.
